name: Razorpay-Style Payment API — Full Production Observability
scenario: Add complete production observability to the Razorpay-style payment API. Kubernetes probes, custom health indicators for the payment gateway, business metrics for every payment event, and a secure Actuator configuration. This is the final polish that makes a Spring Boot API production-ready.
description: PaymentGatewayHealthIndicator (cached, latency-aware). Four Micrometer metrics. Liveness and readiness probes configured. Actuator secured (health+info public, everything else authenticated). @SpringBootTest with 5 tests.

core.description: `PaymentGatewayHealthIndicator` @Component with caching: @Scheduled(fixedRate=30000) refreshes health; health() returns cached AtomicReference<Health>. UP if latency < 500ms, DOWN otherwise. Shows latencyMs in details.
`PaymentMetrics` @Component: Counter payments.processed (with 'currency' tag), Counter payments.failed (with 'reason' tag), AtomicInteger Gauge payments.pending, Timer payment.processing.duration.
`PaymentService.process(PaymentRequest)`: uses all 4 metrics. pending++ on entry, pending-- in finally. processed++ on success, failed++ on exception. wrapped in processingTimer.record().

application.properties: expose health,info,metrics,loggers. show-details=always. management.server.port=8090 (actuator on separate port). info.app.name=payment-service, info.app.version=2.0.0. probes.enabled=true.

@SpringBootTest(RANDOM_PORT) tests (5): /actuator/health UP, /actuator/health/liveness UP, /actuator/health/readiness UP, /actuator/info has version 2.0.0, process() increments payments.processed counter.

bonus.description: Add custom info contributor: InfoContributor that adds current active payment count and total processed since startup to the /actuator/info response.
Secure actuator endpoints: /health and /info public; all others require ADMIN role. Write @SpringBootTest tests verifying /actuator/env returns 401 for unauthenticated and 200 for ADMIN.
Add Prometheus endpoint: spring-boot-starter-actuator + micrometer-registry-prometheus. Expose /actuator/prometheus. @SpringBootTest: verify response contains 'payments_processed_total'.

data_to_use:
Metrics to track:
- payments.processed (counter, tag: currency=INR/USD)
- payments.failed (counter, tag: reason=validation/gateway/timeout)
- payments.pending (gauge — AtomicInteger)
- payment.processing.duration (timer)

Health: PaymentGatewayHealthIndicator — simulate latency 45ms (UP)
Kubernetes probes: /actuator/health/liveness and /actuator/health/readiness

Actuator port: 8090 (in test, use management.server.port from context)
ai_policy_explanation: Limited to 5 hints. Cached HealthIndicator: AtomicReference<Health>, @Scheduled updates it, health() returns it. Separate management port: management.server.port=8090 — TestRestTemplate uses different port (inject @LocalManagementPort). Timer.record(Callable) — must throw Exception from callable, so wrap in try-catch or use Runnable for void.
submission_instructions: Push to GitHub. All 5 @SpringBootTest tests pass. Include screenshot of /actuator/health response showing paymentGateway component.
tips_for_success: AtomicReference<Health>: cachedHealth.set(Health.up()...) in @Scheduled, cachedHealth.get() in health()
Timer.record(Callable<T>) for methods that return values and may throw
management.server.port=8090 and @LocalManagementPort in test for management port
